title: Kanon Test Vector
description: One frozen input paired with the exact verdict a conformant verifier must return, plus the provenance of the rule or attack it encodes. Scoped to x402 v2, scheme exact, EVM, eip3009. Vector format version 2.0.0.

id.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case identifier. Never reused or renumbered.

schema_version.description: Version of this vector format the vector conforms to, as semver.

network.description: CAIP-2 target network the verifier treats as the target, for example eip155:84532.

asset_transfer_method.description: The asset transfer method under test.

encodes.description: The attack or rule in plain language. This is where the human meaningful name lives.

provenance.description: The sources the vector derives from: EIP, CWE, spec clause, or paper. At least one.

description.description: One or two sentences: what the input is and why the verdict is what it is.

input.description: The decoded x402 v2 payment object, stored verbatim. It carries the submitted payload and the accepted requirements together. Field names track the live x402 v2 schema and are not redefined here.

accepted.description: The requirements the client accepted. The EIP-712 domain derives from here: chainId from network, verifyingContract from asset, name and version from extra.

context.description: Injected verification state. Absent means empty context.

verification_time.description: The instant verification is deemed to occur, in unix seconds. Used for validAfter and validBefore.

seen_nonces.description: Nonces already consumed or settled before this verification.

expected.description: The verdict. The reason code is part of the verdict, not a diagnostic afterthought.

valid.description: Whether a conformant verifier must accept the mandate.

reason_code.description: The reason code from the registry. See schema/reason-codes.md.
